title: This New Ultra-Premium Tequila Was Aged for Longer Than Most Bourbons
summary: Codigo 1530 aged this tequila in wine barrels before giving it a Cognac finish.
imageAlt: This New Ultra-Premium Tequila Was Aged for Longer Than Most Bourbons
aiSummary: Codigo 1530 aged this tequila in wine barrels before giving it a Cognac finish.
keyPoints: This New Ultra-Premium Tequila Was Aged for Longer Than Most Bourbons
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de estilo.

multimedia.alt: This New Ultra-Premium Tequila Was Aged for Longer Than Most Bourbons
multimedia.caption: Imagen de portada

fullArticle:
Fifteen years is pretty old for an American whiskey; in fact, most bourbon is bottled at somewhere between four and seven years old. Tequila, on the other hand, usually spends less than three years in barrels. And even when it’s labeled as extra añejo, five years is often the upper limit. Código 1530, however, decided to age its new expression for a full 15 years before giving it a relatively brief finish, making this one of the oldest (and most expensive) tequilas currently on the market.

Código 1530 is technically a celebrity-backed brand, although that’s something it leans on for some releases more than others. It was founded in in 2017 by the former CEO of Crocs Ron Snyder, distiller Federico Vaughn, Betsy Heneghan, and country music star George Strait. In 2022, the brand was acquired...
